note: Personas are living artifacts — they should be updated as real user research, feedback, and testing reveal new insights. These are starting hypotheses, not final definitions.
outsideInValue: These personas represent the people this product must serve. Every capability, design decision, and business rule should be traceable back to at least one persona's pain point or goal.

personas.name: Jordan
personas.description: Agency IT staff with many responsibilities. Managing the application inventory is one of 20 tasks. The annual certification is something to get through — not something Jordan finds valuable.
painPoints: The spreadsheet gets pulled out once a year and filled in from memory — data is stale before it's even submitted
Reminder emails from the state IT authority feel like pressure, not support
No easy way to know what's changed since last year without digging through emails and tickets
Adding a new tool feels like more work, not less
goals: Finish the certification with minimum disruption to other work
Something fast and simple — not another system to learn
Know exactly what needs to be updated without hunting for it
personas.criticalInsight: Jordan's behavior determines whether this product succeeds or fails. If Jordan doesn't keep the inventory updated continuously, everyone else gets bad data. The product must make updating easier than ignoring it.

personas.name: Maria
personas.description: Agency IT lead who signs off on the annual certification. Doesn't do the data entry herself but is accountable for its accuracy. Has been caught off guard by risks she didn't know about.
painPoints: Signs off on certification but doesn't trust the data her team submits
Has been surprised by aging or unsupported systems she wasn't aware of
No visibility into inventory health without asking her team directly
Worried about audit exposure if the data is wrong
goals: Confidence that her agency's inventory is accurate before she signs off
Visibility into risks — aging technology, unsupported versions, AI-enabled apps — without digging into data herself
Be able to act on problems before they become compliance issues
personas.criticalInsight: Maria needs a dashboard that tells her what to worry about — not a spreadsheet she has to interpret herself.

personas.name: Derek
personas.description: State IT authority staff responsible for aggregating agency submissions, cleaning data, and producing portfolio analysis for leadership and legislative reporting.
painPoints: Spends weeks after the certification deadline cleaning inconsistent spreadsheet data before any analysis is possible
Same vendor names spelled 10 different ways across agencies
Fields left blank, retired systems listed as active, wrong lifecycle statuses
By the time the data is clean enough to use, decisions have already been made without it
goals: Receive clean, consistent data so time is spent on analysis — not cleanup
Identify shared service candidates and technology duplication across agencies quickly
Produce reliable portfolio reports for leadership without manual data wrangling
personas.criticalInsight: Derek is the person who sees the full cost of bad data. The product must make data quality automatic — not dependent on agencies doing the right thing voluntarily.

personas.name: Sam
personas.description: A new agency staff member who needs to understand what systems exist, who owns them, and how they relate to the work they've been hired to do.
painPoints: No easy way to find out what systems exist at the agency without asking around
Has to dig through emails, wikis, or old documents to find basic system information
Ownership and contacts for systems are unclear or out of date
goals: Find information about agency systems quickly and without needing to ask someone
Understand who owns a system and who to contact when something is wrong
Get up to speed on the agency's technology landscape without needing a guided tour
personas.criticalInsight: Sam represents a secondary but meaningful value of continuous inventory — it becomes a living reference, not just a compliance artifact.

personas.name: Thomas
personas.description: State-level executive responsible for statewide IT portfolio decisions, funding prioritization, and legislative reporting. Needs reliable data to answer questions from the Governor's office and Legislature.
painPoints: Asked by the Governor's office for statewide views on aging technology and AI adoption — currently based on year-old data
Cannot make confident funding decisions without knowing what agencies actually have
Reports to the Legislature require accurate, timely data that today requires weeks of manual assembly
goals: Timely, reliable data to support funding decisions and legislative reporting
Statewide visibility into technology duplication, AI adoption, and aging infrastructure
Answers without having to manage a tool — just needs accurate information when asked
personas.criticalInsight: Thomas is the reason portfolio intelligence (CAP-07) exists. The value of continuous inventory flows upward — from Jordan updating a record to Thomas answering a question from the Governor's office.
